description_long: This is a large, multi-section suburban house with a complex, L-shaped footprint and a brown gable roof. The exterior is clad in beige horizontal siding, and the structure features multiple rooflines and dormers, giving it a layered, expansive appearance. The front includes a double garage with white paneled doors, a covered porch with columns, and several windows of varying sizes arranged asymmetrically. The rear and side elevations reveal additional entryways, a mix of single and double-hung windows, and a small covered patio area. The house appears to be two stories tall in the main section, with single-story wings extending outward. Architectural details such as overhanging eaves, a prominent entry gable, and a blend of traditional and modern elements suggest a contemporary American suburban style.
description: A large, beige suburban house with a brown gable roof, double garage, and multiple wings. Features include a covered porch, varied window arrangements, and a mix of one- and two-story sections.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double garage, main entry porch with columns, and the primary two-story section.
description_view_1: Top-down rear view highlighting the L-shaped layout, roof complexity, and rear entryway.
description_view_2: Back and side view displaying the two-story section, rear windows, and a small covered patio.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the tall two-story section, window placements, and exterior siding.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry features a covered porch with columns, steps, and a prominent gable overhang.
